title: Theory of Literature
excerpt: The foundational text of the New Criticism and the most important systematic statement of literary theory in the twentieth century, establishing the autonomy of literary study as a discipline.
keyIdeas: Literary study must be distinguished from the history of ideas, sociology, and psychology
The literary work is a stratified structure of meaning, not reducible to paraphrase
Literary theory, criticism, and history are interdependent disciplines
Evaluation is an essential and inescapable dimension of criticism
keyTakeaways: Understand the intrinsic approach to literature that shaped twentieth-century criticism
Recognise the difference between extrinsic and intrinsic methods of analysis
Appreciate the theoretical foundations of the New Criticism and its legacy
whoShouldRead: Students of literary theory seeking the foundations of modern criticism
Scholars interested in the institutional history of English studies
Anyone who wants to understand what it means to study literature as literature
whoShouldSkip: Readers who reject the idea of literature as a distinct object of study
Those seeking a culturally or politically oriented approach to criticism
